sections.title: Overview
sections.content: This guideline covers musculoskeletal risk related to light vehicles, heavy vehicles, site transport, and prolonged driving.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Back, neck, shoulder, or hip pain linked to driving or vehicle vibration
- Symptoms worse after long shifts, rough roads, or repeated entry and exit
- Reduced tolerance to steering, reversing, or seated driving posture

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Vehicle ergonomics includes seat fit, steering and pedal reach, vibration, mirror use, and entry-exit demands
- Driving pain can impair work safety even when it seems minor clinically
- Repeated rough-road exposure can compound recovery problems after injury

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Ask whether driving is currently safe and whether symptoms impair attention, braking, or emergency control
- Advise immediate changes to seat position, rest breaks, or duty modification where needed
- Escalate if pain or neurological symptoms make vehicle operation unsafe

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Assess seat, lumbar support, mirror use, pedal reach, vibration exposure, and entry-exit mechanics
- Recommend route, break, or vehicle modifications where possible
- Link driving restrictions to specific symptoms and review points rather than open-ended bans

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Vehicle ergonomics is about safe control as much as comfort
